title: geraniospermum ceratophyllum
intro: Geraniospermum ceratophyllum, commonly known as the Hooker's Spiked Watermilfoil, is a species of flowering plant in the family Haloragaceae. This plant is known for its potential health benefits, including its use in treating fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues. The therapeutic actions of Geraniospermum ceratophyllum are attributed to its ability to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiseptic properties. Its bioactive constituents include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
intro_study: According to "European review for medical and pharmacological sciences", geraniospermum ceratophyllum (also known as Geranium species) has been shown to have significant antioxidant and antimicrobial benefits due to its high content of polyphenols and hydrolyzable tannins.
side_effects: Geraniospermum ceratophyllum can have side effects if used improperly, such as diarrhea, nausea, and stomach cramps due to its high saponin content. In some cases, excessive consumption can lead to vomiting, headaches, and dizziness. Additionally, the plant's allergy-inducing properties may cause skin irritation, respiratory issues, and anaphylaxis in sensitive individuals. Prolonged use or misuse of Geraniospermum ceratophyllum can also interact with other medications, exacerbating underlying health conditions.
